mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the cross-sectional IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and consumer credit indicate a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenues and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1800 AND RSAFS declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 50% of co-occurring trigger events, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects expanding capacity utilization and supply-side efficiency gains, which directly boost corporate earnings and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 0.5% during periods of rising INDPRO, or the rolling Spearman correlation falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth serves as a direct proxy for aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue scaling, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions trend upward.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive relationship with GDPC1 growth rates over any 3-year rolling window (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces monetary authorities to tighten policy, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuation multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during high-inflation months, or the predictive Rank IC fails to exceed 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates indicate robust labor market demand without wage-push inflation, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the multivariate regression R-squared drops below 0.01 over a 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts act as a leading indicator for broader construction and consumer durable demand, amplifying industrial output growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon when housing activity exceeds historical norms.
failure_condition: Forward returns fail to outperform the baseline by at least 0.8% when the trigger activates, or the cross-correlation between HOUST/INDPRO growth and SPX returns becomes negative over a 3-year rolling window.
